title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text: In the symmetric three-phase load connected to a three-phase four-wire power supply at the industrial frequency shown in the figure, the line voltage of the power supply is 380 V, and the clamp ammeter reads 4.4 A. If points B and C are simultaneously open-circuited and all other points are normal, what will the clamp ammeter read (in A)?
expected_answer: When points B and C are simultaneously open-circuited and other points are normal, the clamp ammeter reads 0 A.

title: EEE-Bench 子题 1
rewritten_question_text:
A 3-phase, 440 V, 50 Hz, 4-pole slip ring induction motor is fed from the rotor side through an auto-transformer, and the stator is connected to a variable resistance as shown in the figure.

The motor is coupled to a 220 V, separately excited D.C. generator feeding power to a fixed resistance of 10 \( \Omega \). Two-wattmeter method is used to measure the input power to the induction motor. The variable resistance is adjusted so that the motor runs at 1410 rpm, and the following readings were recorded: \( W_1 = 1800 \, W \), \( W_2 = -200 \, W \).

Neglecting all losses of both machines, what is the DC generator power output?

title: EEE-Bench 子题 2
rewritten_question_text:
A 3-phase, 440 V, 50 Hz, 4-pole slip ring induction motor is fed from the rotor side through an auto-transformer, and the stator is connected to a variable resistance as shown in the figure.

The motor is coupled to a 220 V, separately excited D.C. generator feeding power to a fixed resistance of 10 \( \Omega \). Two-wattmeter method is used to measure the input power to the induction motor. The variable resistance is adjusted so that the motor runs at 1410 rpm, and the following readings were recorded: \( W_1 = 1800 \, W \), \( W_2 = -200 \, W \).

Neglecting all losses of both machines, what is the current through resistance \( R_{exc} \)?

rewritten_question_text: For the BJT in the amplifier shown in the image, \(V_{BE}=0.7\text{ V}\), \(kT/q=26\text{ mV}\). Assume the BJT output resistance \((r_o)\) is very high and the base current is negligible. The capacitors are also assumed to be short-circuited at signal frequencies. The input \(v_i\) is direct coupled. What is the low-frequency gain \(v_o/v_i\) of the amplifier?
